api: Amplitude Analytics REST APIs (https://amplitude.com/docs/apis) — HTTP V2 (event ingestion), Behavioral Cohorts, Chart Annotations, Taxonomy, Export
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against the live docs (https://amplitude.com/docs/apis/analytics/behavioral-cohorts, .../chart-annotations, .../taxonomy, .../export, .../http-v2, fetched 2026-07-04). Every practical GET list/detail resource is now a stream (cohorts, cohorts_usage, annotations, annotation_categories, events_list, taxonomy_categories, taxonomy_events, taxonomy_event_properties, taxonomy_user_properties, taxonomy_group_properties) and every dialect-expressible mutation is a writes.json action (annotation CRUD, annotation-category CRUD, taxonomy category CUD, taxonomy event CUD). Excluded endpoints fall into a few real categories: cohort membership download/upload (a multi-step async job-poll + presigned-S3-redirect protocol, not a plain request/response the read/write dialect can express), the raw event Export API (returns a zipped multi-file binary archive, not JSON), event ingestion (a write-only telemetry sink, not a reverse-ETL mutation of an existing resource), and a deliberate write-depth cut below the event/category level of the taxonomy (event-property, user-property, and group-property CUD are read-only in this wave; only the read streams are covered for those 3 resources) — see individual entries for exact rationale.

excluded.reason: step 1 of Amplitude's 2-step async cohort-membership download protocol (returns a request_id/cohort_id pair, not the membership data itself); the dialect has no async-job-poll-then-fetch primitive to express the request/request-status/file 3-endpoint sequence as a single declarative stream
step 2 of the async cohort-membership download protocol; polls job status, no standalone record value without the paired request/file endpoints

returns either a small inline body or a 302 redirect to a presigned S3 URL for large cohorts; the redirect-to-external-storage shape has no declarative equivalent and the payload itself is a bulk id list, not a projectable record shape

bulk-uploads an arbitrary user/amplitude-id list to create or replace cohort membership wholesale; a write action with no path-addressable existing record (a cohort id is optional/generated) and payload shape (an id array, potentially large) is a bulk-import operation outside the per-record create/update/delete write model

incrementally adds/removes members to/from cohort membership via an array of {ids, id_type, operation} objects; same bulk-membership-list shape as cohorts/upload, not a single addressable record mutation
single-annotation detail GET; every field it returns is already present on each record of the annotations list stream, which this connector reads to exhaustion every sync
single-category detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the annotation_categories list stream

bulk-reassigns an array of annotation_ids to a category in one call; a batch-of-ids mutation, not a single addressable record the per-record write model targets — an operator can express the same effect as N update_annotation calls setting each annotation's category individually
single-category detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the taxonomy_categories list stream
single-event-type detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the taxonomy_events list stream

undoes a prior soft-delete; a state-transition action on an already-deleted resource with no corresponding read/list of deleted event types to source a record from, outside the create/update/delete write model
single-property detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the taxonomy_event_properties list stream
event-property mutations were not prioritized for this wave (read coverage was); a follow-up capability expansion can add create/update/delete once a reverse-ETL use case is scoped
same rationale as the create action above
same rationale as the create action above
restore action for a mutation surface not yet covered (event-property CUD)
single-property detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the taxonomy_user_properties list stream
user-property mutations were not prioritized for this wave (read coverage was); a follow-up capability expansion can add create/update/delete once a reverse-ETL use case is scoped
same rationale as the create action above
same rationale as the create action above
restore action for a mutation surface not yet covered (user-property CUD)
single-property detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the taxonomy_group_properties list stream
group-property mutations were not prioritized for this wave (read coverage was); a follow-up capability expansion can add create/update once a reverse-ETL use case is scoped
same rationale as the create action above

returns a zipped archive of newline-delimited JSON event files (not a single JSON body); the read dialect has no gzip/zip decompression or archive-member-fan-out primitive (conventions.md §1's Tier-2 trigger list names response decompression/CSV parsing as a hook-only concern, and this connector deliberately stays Tier-1 with zero hooks)

event-ingestion telemetry sink (creates NEW raw analytics events from a client SDK), not a reverse-ETL mutation of an existing Amplitude-managed resource; out of scope for a connector whose write surface targets annotations/taxonomy
